description:
Cookie consent implementation **plus** an analytics vendor decision that respects our BAA boundary. The privacy policy already documents cookies; nothing currently implements consent.

**Scope**
- Cookie consent banner — opt-in for non-essential cookies, persisted as a `consents` row (kind: `cookies`, versioned per T-018b infrastructure ticket).
- Analytics vendor: avoid putting PHI-adjacent identifiers into a non-BAA tool. Options ranked:
  1. **PostHog Cloud (US) with BAA on Teams tier** (recommended once volume justifies; until then, self-hosted PostHog on the same AWS account is BAA-clean by default).
  2. Plausible (privacy-first, no BAA needed but limited event model).
  3. Posthog OSS self-hosted on our AWS infra (BAA-clean since it's inside our boundary; ops overhead).
- All analytics calls go through a `src/lib/analytics/client.ts` facade. Patient identifier is `apoth_user_id` (opaque); never email or name.
- Suppress analytics in `/onboarding/identity/**`, `/onboarding/intake/**`, `/dashboard/**` if vendor doesn't have BAA.

**Acceptance**
- Banner appears on first visit; non-essential cookies blocked until consent.
- `consents` row written on accept/decline with version.
- Analytics tag fires only on marketing surfaces in the no-BAA path.
title: Analytics & cookie consent — BAA-aware vendor selection
